name: Haryana
stopoverNote: Haryana sits directly on NH-44 between Delhi and Chandigarh. Panipat is only 90 km from Delhi and Kurukshetra is 160 km, so the heritage corridor can be driven as Delhi NCR day-trips; overnighting in Kurukshetra and Pinjore/Morni Hills makes the route more immersive. Chandigarh is the natural exit for Himachal Pradesh or Punjab.

diet.vegetarian: Excellent for vegetarians. Haryanvi food is built around bajra roti, missi roti, saag, dal, kachri chutney and filling vegetarian thalis; even small-town dhabas usually serve veg meals for ₹80–₹150.
diet.vegan: Dairy is central to Haryanvi food: lassi, ghee, dahi, kadhi, kheer, malpua and churma are common. Vegans must clearly ask for 'bina ghee, bina dahi, bina doodh' at every meal; Gurugram has more vegan-friendly cafés, but Kurukshetra, Panipat and Morni Hills are limited. Safer choices are plain sabzi, dal, kachri chutney and tandoori roti without ghee.
phrases: Bina dahi/doodh = without curd or milk
Sirf tel mein banao = cook only in oil

cities.name: Panipat
cities.notes: Three decisive battles shaped Indian history on these plains; best as a compact half-day heritage stop between Delhi and Kurukshetra.

cities.name: Kurukshetra
cities.notes: Mahabharata battlefield and Bhagavad Gita pilgrimage city, anchored by Brahma Sarovar and Jyotisar.

cities.name: Pinjore
cities.notes: A half-day heritage stop for Yadavindra Mughal Gardens and the open-air Bhima Devi temple sculpture museum near Panchkula.

cities.name: Morni Hills
cities.notes: Haryana's only hill station — twin lakes, a ruined hilltop fort and modest Shivalik adventure activities after the heritage corridor.

itinerary.sim: Any Indian prepaid SIM (Jio or Airtel) works well across NH-44, Panipat, Kurukshetra, Pinjore and Panchkula. Coverage is generally strong; Morni Hills can have weak data in valleys, so download maps before the climb.

Panipat.name: Panipat
Panipat.note: A morning arrival from Delhi covers the key battle monuments by early afternoon; overnight only if textile shopping matters.

days.note: Arrive by 9am from Delhi. The main sites are within roughly 5 km, so this is a realistic half-day before continuing to Kurukshetra.

activities.name: Ibrahim Lodhi Tomb
activities.tip: Tomb of the last Sultan of Delhi, defeated in the First Battle of Panipat in 1526; go first while the garden is quiet.

activities.name: Kabuli Bagh Mosque & Garden
activities.tip: Built by Babur after his 1526 victory; one of North India's early Mughal mosques and only about 200m from Ibrahim Lodhi Tomb.

activities.name: Panipat Museum
activities.tip: Closed Mondays; timings are roughly 10am–5pm. Visit before the battlefield memorials for battle maps, weapons and context.

activities.name: Kala Amb Memorial
activities.tip: Small garden memorial linked to the Second Battle of Panipat; timings are typically 9am–6pm.

activities.name: Devi Temple & Panipat Textile Market
activities.tip: Panipat is the City of Weavers; the textile market is best Monday–Saturday mornings if you want fabrics or home textiles.

hotels.name: Budget hotels near Panipat bus stand

Kurukshetra.name: Kurukshetra
Kurukshetra.note: Two days is honest: one for Brahma Sarovar, temples and museums; one for Jyotisar, Thanesar and quieter Mahabharata sites.

days.theme: Sacred waters of the Kuru land
days.note: Start at sunrise for Brahma Sarovar aarti. Most sights are within a short radius, but keep the Jyotisar show for the evening.

activities.name: Brahma Sarovar — sunrise aarti & morning walk
activities.tip: Open roughly 5am–9pm. The tank circuit is about 3.6 km; Oct–Feb brings migratory birds.

activities.name: Sannihit Sarovar & Sthaneshwar Mahadev Temple
activities.tip: About 2 km from Brahma Sarovar; expect a more local pilgrimage feel than the headline tank.

activities.name: Sri Krishna Museum
activities.tip: Closed Mondays, usually 10am–4:15pm; photography is not allowed inside galleries.

activities.name: Kurukshetra Panorama & Science Centre
activities.tip: The 360-degree battlefield cyclorama pairs well with Sri Krishna Museum next door.

activities.name: Jyotisar evening Light & Sound Show
activities.tip: Arrive 30 minutes early. During Gita Mahotsav, an additional Vishwaroop projection-mapping show may run.

days.note: Sites are spread 5–15 km from town. Hire an auto for ₹400–₹600 or a cab; avoid Harsha Ka Tila in heavy monsoon mud.

activities.name: Jyotisar sacred grove by daylight
activities.tip: See the sacred tree, stone chariot and temple calmly; it feels different from the evening show.

activities.name: Bhishma Kund (Narkatari Tirth)
activities.tip: Small tank associated with Bhishma Pitamaha; less crowded and atmospheric.

activities.name: Harsha Ka Tila, Thanesar
activities.tip: Outdoor ASI mound linked to Harsha's 7th-century capital; infrastructure is limited but the history is strong.

activities.name: Birla Gita Mandir & Sheikh Chilli's Tomb
activities.tip: Both sit near Brahma Sarovar; the tomb is a well-kept Mughal-era structure with a small museum.

activities.name: Local Haryanvi thali lunch
activities.tip: Try bajra roti with ghee, kadhi-rice and lassi; vegans must ask for bina ghee and bina dahi.

Pinjore.name: Pinjore
Pinjore.note: Pinjore's two major attractions are adjacent, so it is best as a half-day stop before Morni Hills or Chandigarh.

days.note: Visit the gardens in early light, then walk next door to Bhima Devi Museum; continue onward by afternoon if you are not staying in Chandigarh.

activities.name: Yadavindra Gardens (Pinjore Gardens)
activities.tip: Open roughly 7am–9:30pm. Seven terraced levels, fountains and palace pavilions are most photogenic from 7–10am; evening illumination is worthwhile if staying late.

activities.name: Bhima Devi Temple Museum
activities.tip: Closed Mondays, usually 9am–5pm. This open-air sculpture park displays 8th–12th century temple fragments and is genuinely undervisited.

activities.name: Mughal-era Shish Mahal
activities.tip: A small mirrored palace inside the garden complex; worth a slow look for architectural details.

activities.name: Pinjore Heritage Festival
activities.tip: Seasonal festival around Baisakhi/April in some years; verify 2026 dates before planning around it.

activities.name: Pinjore–Chandigarh dhaba snack stop
activities.tip: The border-road dhabas are good for Amritsari kulcha or chai before the Morni climb.

Morni Hills.name: Morni Hills
Morni Hills.note: Morni is modest but refreshing. Stay overnight for sunrise; roads are narrow and should be avoided in heavy monsoon.

days.note: Arrive by noon from Pinjore. Adventure activities close by early evening, and the fort path needs proper shoes.

activities.name: Tikkar Taal — boating & water sports
activities.tip: The package may include boating, kayaking and rope activities; confirm what is operating on arrival.

activities.name: Morni Fort hilltop walk
activities.tip: The 30–45 minute uphill walk reaches ruined fort walls and twin-lake views. Avoid fog, heavy rain and slippery monsoon paths.

activities.name: Morni Village walk & local market
activities.tip: Look for local honey and pickles; carry cash as ATMs are limited.

activities.name: Chidiyapur Lake
activities.tip: Quieter than Tikkar Taal and good for winter bird photography.

meals: Carry snacks and water from Panchkula

days.note: A light half-day makes the overnight worthwhile before descending toward Chandigarh airport or railway station.

activities.name: Sunrise nature walk / Shivalik trail
activities.tip: Leave around 6am for a 2–3 km forest trail; watch for monkeys, deer and winter birds.

activities.name: Breakfast at resort or dhaba
activities.tip: Depart by 10–11am if connecting onward from Chandigarh.

activities.name: Optional second stop at Tikkar Taal viewpoint
activities.tip: Good final photo stop in clear weather before the descent.

connections.method: Road by cab or Haryana Roadways bus on NH-44

connections.cost: ₹100–₹150 bus with change at Ambala; ₹1,200–₹1,800 private cab

connections.method: Pre-booked cab or own vehicle on hilly road
connections.cost: ₹600–₹900 private cab; limited direct bus service

extras: This is an honest 4–5 day add-on, not a marquee standalone circuit: many Haryana sights work as Delhi NCR or Chandigarh day-trips.
Recommended route: Delhi → Panipat → Kurukshetra → Pinjore → Morni Hills → Chandigarh, about 350 km total including entry/exit drives.
International Gita Mahotsav usually runs late November to early December; Gita Jayanti in 2026 falls on December 20. Book Kurukshetra rooms early around festival dates.
Faridabad's Surajkund Mela is excellent only when running; the 2026 edition ran January 31–February 15 with entry around ₹120 weekdays / ₹180 weekends.
Sultanpur National Park is a strong birding half-day from Delhi or Gurugram, with ₹5 Indian adult entry, but it is off the main Panipat–Kurukshetra–Morni corridor and closes June 1–September 30.
Gurugram's Kingdom of Dreams is not an active attraction: it has been closed since 2022 and was gutted by fire in March 2025.
April–June heat can reach 40–46°C; July–September monsoon makes Morni roads slippery and outdoor sightseeing unpleasant.
Entry fees are tiny; budget is dominated by transport, hotels and food. Carry cash for Morni Hills and small-town sites.
